message:
chore: rustfmt cleanup on stream cancel thread + beads sync

- Fix indentation in provider_stream_simple_cancel_best_effort thread
  closure to match rustfmt expectations
- Sync beads issue tracker state

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1nq","title":"Integrate extension conformance harness into tests + CI","description":"Background:
- Conformance must be automated and gated.

Steps:
- Add harness tests to `cargo test` default set.
- Document how to run just extension conformance.
- Update CI workflow to include these tests and capture logs as artifacts.

Acceptance:
- CI fails on extension conformance regressions with clear logs and downloadable artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
- [ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
- [ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
- [ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
- [ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
- [ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes

 {"id":"bd-1o4","title":"E2E CLI scenarios: tool enable/disable + error paths","description":"Goal:
- Add E2E tests that validate tool enable/disable behavior and common error paths with detailed logging.

Scope:
- `--tools` selection impacts available tool list in system prompt.
- `--no-tools` disables all tools and confirms tool-call failures are surfaced.
- Error paths: invalid model/provider, missing API key, rpc mode restrictions.
- Validate that error messages are clear and actionable.

Logging Requirements:
- Use CLI E2E harness (bd-1wc) + TestLogger (bd-3ml).
- Log effective tool list, system prompt excerpt, VCR_MODE, and cassette name/path.
- Capture stdout/stderr and assertion diffs on failure.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Tests capture stdout/stderr and confirm error messages.
- Logs show tool registry inputs + effective tool list + prompt excerpts + cassette id.
- No network access; VCR playback used where streaming needed.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

 {"id":"bd-1oj9","title":"Implement pi.registerProvider() for custom LLM providers","description":"Phase 1: Allow extensions to register custom LLM providers with standard APIs.

## API Spec (Phase 1 - Basic Registration)
```typescript
pi.registerProvider(name, {
  baseUrl: string,
  apiKey: string,  // env var name
  api: 'anthropic-messages' | 'openai-responses',
  models: Model[]
});
```

## Scope (Phase 1 Only)
This task implements basic provider registration using standard API types.
Advanced features are in separate phases:
- Phase 2 (bd-39q8): Custom streamSimple handler
- Phase 3 (bd-3my9): OAuth support

## Implementation Requirements
1. ProviderRegistry in extension runtime
2. Hostcall handler for 'registerProvider'
3. Integration with provider system (src/provider.rs)
4. Integration with model registry (src/models.rs)
5. Validation of api type (anthropic-messages | openai-responses)

## Test Plan
- Unit test: registerProvider stores provider config
- Unit test: invalid api type rejected
- Integration test: registered provider appears in --list-models
- E2E test: pi --provider custom-provider uses registered provider

## Files to Modify
- src/extensions.rs (ProviderRegistry)
- src/extensions_js.rs (hostcall handler)
- src/provider.rs (integrate extension providers)
- src/models.rs (add extension provider models)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:10:14.120496236Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T04:43:07.524435608Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T04:43:07.524372070Z","close_reason":"Fully implemented: JS bridge __pi_register_provider (extensions_js.rs:2796-2838) with validation for id/models/baseUrl/apiKey/api, Rust hostcall handler registerprovider (extensions.rs:5808-5851) with api type validation, and manager.register_provider() for persistence.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1oj9","depends_on_id":"bd-2bnc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:10:14.128100916Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1oj9","depends_on_id":"bd-37qz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:13:34.441437607Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-24p5","title":"Implement pi.exec() for shell execution from extensions","description":"Allow extensions to execute shell commands.

## API Spec (from legacy)
```typescript
await pi.exec(
  command: string,
  args: string[],
  options?: {
    cwd?: string;
    env?: Record<string, string>;
    timeout?: number;
  }
): Promise<{
  code: number;
  stdout: string;
  stderr: string;
}>
```

## Use Cases
- Git operations from extensions
- Build/test automation
- File system operations beyond basic tools
- Custom tooling integration

## Implementation Requirements
1. Hostcall handler for 'exec'
2. Spawn subprocess with command, args, options
3. Capture stdout/stderr
4. Apply timeout if specified
5. Return result to JS

## Security Considerations
- Extensions execute with user's permissions
- Timeout prevents runaway processes
- Consider capability gating (strict policy might block)

## Implementation Notes
- Use asupersync::process or std::process::Command
- Stream output to rolling buffer
- Kill process tree on timeout

## Test Plan
- Unit test: simple command executes and returns
- Unit test: timeout kills process
- Unit test: env vars passed correctly
- Integration test: extension can run git commands

## Files to Modify
- src/extensions.rs (hostcall handler)
- Possibly reuse logic from bash tool","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:11:43.643526706Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T04:02:04.379894327Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T04:02:04.379819357Z","close_reason":"Already implemented: dispatch_exec handler in extension_dispatcher.rs:134-276 handles cmd, args, cwd, timeout, stdout/stderr capture, and process tree killing. JS API wired via __pi_exec in extensions_js.rs.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-24p5","depends_on_id":"bd-2ca4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:11:43.653512273Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-24p5","depends_on_id":"bd-37qz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:13:40.900406172Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-254u","title":"Task: Implement get_registered_tools() in PiJsRuntime","description":"# Task: Implement get_registered_tools() in PiJsRuntime

## Objective

Add a method to PiJsRuntime that queries the JS runtime for all tools registered by extensions.

## Background

Extensions register tools in JS via `pi.registerTool()`. These are stored in the JS registry object. We need to access this from Rust to build the tool list for the agent.

## Implementation

### JS Side (in PI_BRIDGE_JS)

The registry already exists:
```javascript
const __pi_registry = {
    tools: new Map(),
    commands: new Map(),
    // ...
};

pi.registerTool = function(config) {
    __pi_registry.tools.set(config.name, config);
};
```

Add a getter function:
```javascript
globalThis.__pi_get_registered_tools = function() {
    const tools = [];
    for (const [name, config] of __pi_registry.tools) {
        tools.push({
            name: config.name,
            label: config.label || config.name,
            description: config.description || "",
            parameters: config.parameters || { type: "object", properties: {} },
        });
    }
    return JSON.stringify(tools);
};
```

### Rust Side

```rust
impl PiJsRuntime {
    /// Get all tools registered by extensions.
    /// 
    /// Returns a list of tool definitions that can be wrapped
    /// and added to the agent's tool registry.
    pub async fn get_registered_tools(&self) -> Result<Vec<ExtensionToolDef>> {
        let result = self.eval("__pi_get_registered_tools()").await?;
        
        let tools_json = result.as_str()
            .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("Expected string from __pi_get_registered_tools"))?;
        
        let tools: Vec<ExtensionToolDef> = serde_json::from_str(tools_json)?;
        Ok(tools)
    }
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize)]
pub struct ExtensionToolDef {
    pub name: String,
    pub label: String,
    pub description: String,
    pub parameters: serde_json::Value,
}
```

## Testing

1. Unit test: No tools registered → empty list
2. Unit test: One tool registered → list of 1
3. Unit test: Multiple tools → all returned
4. Unit test: Tool definition fields correct

## Dependencies

- Part of: bd-1yo9 (Extension Tool Registration)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] __pi_get_registered_tools() added to JS bridge
- [ ] get_registered_tools() method in PiJsRuntime
- [ ] Returns correct ExtensionToolDef structs
- [ ] Unit tests pass","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:59:14.738645483Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T20:47:18.173458515Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T20:47:18.173374799Z","close_reason":"Already implemented + tested in src/extensions_js.rs (get_registered_tools + __pi_get_registered_tools)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-254u","depends_on_id":"bd-1yo9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:59:14.750376573Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":235,"issue_id":"bd-254u","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Blocked for now:  is under an exclusive file reservation by RedSpring (requested access via Agent Mail). Will resume once reservation is released or expires.","created_at":"2026-02-04T20:43:52Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-25n4","title":"Editor: ! and !! bash shortcuts","description":"# Goal
Implement legacy bash shortcut behavior in interactive mode:
- `!command` runs the command and sends its output to the LLM.
- `!!command` runs the command but does **not** send its output to the LLM.

# Legacy Notes
From `legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/README.md`.
Legacy also changes editor border color when the editor content starts with `!`.

# Required Behavior
- Commands run via the same underlying runner as the `bash` tool (timeout, kill process tree, capture output).
- For `!cmd`:
  - render output in chat
  - include output in context as a user message (or a structured message type) so the model sees it
- For `!!cmd`:
  - render output in chat
  - do not add output to model context

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `!` and `!!` behave differently with respect to context.
- [ ] Timeouts and errors are handled safely and visibly.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic

 {"id":"bd-25q","title":"Extension compatibility proof: popular sample + conformance harness","description":"Goal:
- Prove that a large, diverse, *popular* sample of existing pi-agent extensions runs "as-is" with full behavioral fidelity in pi_agent_rust.
- Produce evidence that is testable, repeatable, and automated (fixtures + harness + reports).

Scope notes:
- "As-is" means no changes to extension source code or packaging, except for standard install/build steps that the legacy pi toolchain already requires.
- Compatibility must be demonstrated across tools, slash commands, and event hooks where provided.
- Evidence must include deterministic conformance fixtures and a runner that compares Rust output to legacy output.

Key references (for future self):
- EXTENSIONS.md (design goals, runtime tiers).
- docs/schema/extension_protocol.json + docs/wit/extension.wit (protocol spec).
- EXISTING_PI_STRUCTURE.md sections 7, 9, 10 for CLI/resource flow.

Exit criteria:
- Workstream summaries (sample, capture, runtime, harness, benchmarks, docs) are each marked done.
- The conformance harness passes for the entire selected sample.
- A written report maps each extension to a pass/fail status and notes any deviations.

 {"id":"bd-340x","title":"Message queue: keybindings + editor interactions","description":"# Goal
Implement the keybinding-driven UX for queueing and dequeuing messages.

# Required Behavior (legacy)
- Enter while busy: enqueue **steering** message.
- Alt+Enter while busy: enqueue **follow-up** message.
- Escape while busy: abort current work and restore queued messages into editor.
- Alt+Up: dequeue queued messages back into editor for editing.

# Dependencies
- Requires keybinding action layer (`bd-3ip`).

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Shortcuts work even when focus is not strictly the editor (as long as interactive is active).
- [ ] Dequeue restores messages in the correct order.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

 {"id":"bd-346","title":"Workstream: Session Index Integration","description":"# Workstream: Session Index Integration

## Goal
Wire the existing SQLite session index (src/session_index.rs) into the CLI flow
for fast session discovery and resumption.

## Background
The session index module is fully implemented (360+ lines) but not used:
- SQLite-based index with WAL mode
- Fast lookups by path or CWD
- Session metadata extraction (message count, name, file size, mtime)
- Atomic operations with file locking

Currently, session operations scan the filesystem directly, which is slow
for users with many sessions.

## Current State

### What Exists (src/session_index.rs)
```rust
pub struct SessionIndex { db: SqliteConnection }

impl SessionIndex {
    pub fn open(path: &Path) -> Result<Self>
    pub fn index_session(&self, session_path: &Path) -> Result<()>
    pub fn list_sessions(&self, cwd: Option<&str>) -> Result<Vec<SessionMeta>>
    pub fn find_recent(&self, cwd: &str) -> Result<Option<SessionMeta>>
    pub fn reindex_all(&self) -> Result<()>
}

pub struct SessionMeta {
    pub path: PathBuf,
    pub cwd: String,
    pub name: Option<String>,
    pub message_count: u32,
    pub created_at: DateTime,
    pub modified_at: DateTime,
    pub file_size: u64,
}
```

### What's Missing
1. No calls to `index_session()` after session.persist()
2. No calls to `list_sessions()` in session picker
3. No calls to `find_recent()` for --continue flag
4. No index maintenance hooks

## Implementation Plan

### Phase 1: Wire Index on Session Save
- After session.persist(), call session_index.index_session()
- Index path stored at ~/.pi/agent/sessions/index.db

### Phase 2: Use Index for Session Listing
- --resume flag queries index instead of filesystem walk
- Session picker uses indexed metadata
- Much faster for users with 100+ sessions

### Phase 3: Use Index for --continue
- --continue queries index for most recent by CWD
- Falls back to filesystem if index empty/stale

### Phase 4: Index Maintenance
- Background reindex on startup (if stale)
- Handle deleted sessions (prune index)
- Handle moved sessions (update paths)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Sessions indexed on save
- [ ] Session picker uses index
- [ ] --continue uses index
- [ ] Startup remains fast (<100ms)
- [ ] Handles index corruption gracefully

## Files Affected
- src/session_index.rs (already implemented)
- src/session.rs (add indexing calls)
- src/session_picker.rs (use index queries)
- src/main.rs (wire up index)","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] All child/dependent beads include unit tests + E2E scripts with JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9 (or N/A with rationale) and are closed

 {"id":"bd-35hz","title":"Task: Implement tool_call event dispatch (blocking)","description":"# Task: Implement tool_call Event Dispatch (Blocking)

## Objective

Dispatch the tool_call event before tool execution, allowing extensions to block or observe tool calls.

## Background

The tool_call event is critical for extension-based tool interception:
- Security: Block dangerous commands
- Monitoring: Log all tool usage
- Modification: Transform inputs (future enhancement)

## TypeScript Reference (wrapper.ts:48-66)

```typescript
if (runner.hasHandlers("tool_call")) {
    const callResult = await runner.emitToolCall({
        type: "tool_call",
        toolName: tool.name,
        toolCallId,
        input: params,
    });

    if (callResult?.block) {
        const reason = callResult.reason || "Tool execution was blocked by an extension";
        throw new Error(reason);
    }
}
```

## Implementation

### In Agent Tool Execution

```rust
impl Agent {
    async fn execute_tool(
        &self,
        tool_call: &ToolCall,
        event_dispatcher: Option<&EventDispatcher>,
    ) -> Result<ToolOutput> {
        // Dispatch tool_call event before execution
        if let Some(dispatcher) = event_dispatcher {
            if dispatcher.has_handlers("tool_call") {
                let event = ExtensionEvent::ToolCall {
                    tool_name: tool_call.name.clone(),
                    tool_call_id: tool_call.id.clone(),
                    input: tool_call.arguments.clone(),
                };
                
                if let Some(result) = dispatcher.dispatch::<ToolCallEventResult>(event).await? {
                    if result.block {
                        let reason = result.reason.unwrap_or_else(||
                            "Tool execution was blocked by an extension".to_string()
                        );
                        return Err(PiError::ToolBlocked { 
                            tool: tool_call.name.clone(),
                            reason,
                        });
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        
        // Proceed with tool execution
        let tool = self.tools.get(&tool_call.name)
            .ok_or_else(|| PiError::ToolNotFound(tool_call.name.clone()))?;
        
        tool.execute(&tool_call.id, tool_call.arguments.clone(), None).await
    }
}
```

### Error Handling

When a tool is blocked:
1. Create ToolResultMessage with is_error=true
2. Content: "Tool execution blocked: {reason}"
3. Don't execute the tool
4. Continue agent loop (model will see error)

## Event Data Format

```json
{
    "type": "tool_call",
    "toolName": "bash",
    "toolCallId": "call-abc123",
    "input": {
        "command": "rm -rf /"
    }
}
```

## Handler Example (Extension)

```javascript
ctx.on("tool_call", async (event) => {
    if (event.toolName === "bash" && event.input.command.includes("rm -rf")) {
        return { block: true, reason: "Dangerous command blocked" };
    }
    return {}; // Allow
});
```

## Testing

1. Unit test: No handlers → proceeds normally
2. Unit test: Handler returns empty → proceeds
3. Unit test: Handler returns block=true → tool blocked
4. Unit test: Handler throws → error logged, proceed
5. Integration test: Extension blocks dangerous bash command

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-tg4w (EventDispatcher)
- Depends on: bd-jt3k (Agent loop integration)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] tool_call event dispatched before execution
- [ ] Blocking returns proper error
- [ ] Non-blocking proceeds normally
- [ ] Handler errors don't crash agent
- [ ] Unit tests pass","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:56:47.904048705Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T00:43:26.056339588Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T00:43:26.056276942Z","close_reason":"Dispatch tool_call before tool exec with blocking semantics + tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-35hz","depends_on_id":"bd-10vp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:56:47.915924536Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35hz","depends_on_id":"bd-jt3k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:57:10.890321554Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35hz","depends_on_id":"bd-tg4w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:57:09.671931893Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-35y0","title":"Interactive: startup header (hints/resources/changelog)","description":"# Goal
Implement a rich startup header similar to legacy.

# Required Content
- App name + version.
- Keybinding hints for core actions:
  - interrupt, clear, exit, suspend
  - cycle thinking, cycle models, select model
  - expand tools, toggle thinking
  - external editor
  - “/ for commands”, “! to run bash”, “!! to run bash (no context)”, “Ctrl+V to paste image”, “drop files to attach”

- Loaded resources summary:
  - AGENTS files loaded
  - prompt templates
  - skills
  - themes
  - extensions (just counts/paths; do not implement extension runtime here)

- Changelog:
  - If new entries exist, show “What’s New” (or condensed if `collapse_changelog` true)

# Settings
- Respect:
  - `quiet_startup`
  - `collapse_changelog`

# Dependencies
- Keybinding hints require keybindings (`bd-3ip`).

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Header appears on startup and is suppressible.
- [ ] Changelog behavior matches settings.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
